subject: Weekly status doc — can you send it over?
snippet: Hey, did you ever finish that weekly status doc you mentioned last night? I know...
body:
Hey, did you ever finish that weekly status doc you mentioned last night? I know you said you were going to send it to the team this morning but I haven't seen it hit the shared drive yet.

Just a heads up — Mom asked if she could see what you've been working on and I told her I'd forward something. Nothing confidential, just a high-level thing if you have it.

No rush, but sooner is better. Thanks!

— Sarah

subject: Re: Weekly status doc — can you send it over?
snippet: Ha, yes — I literally just finished it ten minutes ago. Was waiting on two numbe...
body:
Ha, yes — I literally just finished it ten minutes ago. Was waiting on two numbers from the product team before I could call it done. It's uploading to the shared drive now, should be there within the next few minutes under "Team Updates > June 2026."

For your mom I'd probably just screenshot the summary section on page one, the rest is pretty inside-baseball. I can also just type up a quick two-liner if that's easier for her.

Sorry for the delay — this week has been a lot. See you at dinner.

— Brian

subject: Lunch this week?
snippet: Hey Brian, been a while since we grabbed lunch. You free Thursday or Friday arou...
body:
Hey Brian, been a while since we grabbed lunch. You free Thursday or Friday around noon? Was thinking we could hit that taco place on 5th — the one we went to after the playoff game last year. Pretty sure they still have those street corn nachos you wouldn't shut up about.

Let me know what works. If this week's too crazy we can push to next.

subject: Re: Lunch this week?
snippet: Dude yes, I've actually been thinking about that place too. Thursday works great...
body:
Dude yes, I've actually been thinking about that place too. Thursday works great for me — Friday I've got a 1pm call that always runs long so I'd rather not risk it.

Thursday noon at Taqueria 5th sounds perfect. Do you want to just meet there or are you coming from downtown? I can swing by and pick you up if it's easier.

subject: Re: Lunch this week?
snippet: Thursday noon is locked in. I'll just meet you there, I'm actually working from ...
body:
Thursday noon is locked in. I'll just meet you there, I'm actually working from the coffee shop on Weston that morning so it's basically on my way.

Also forewarning — I'm going to order the entire left side of the menu so bring your appetite. See you Thursday!

subject: Design Review Request – Kitchen Renovation Concepts
snippet: Hey, I finally got the mood boards back from the designer and I'd love for you t...
body:
Hey, I finally got the mood boards back from the designer and I'd love for you to take a look before we give her the green light. There are two concepts — one leans more modern with the waterfall island, and the other keeps it more transitional with open shelving. Both are within budget but I want to make sure you're actually happy with the direction before we commit.

Can you carve out like 20 minutes this week to go through them together? I was thinking Wednesday evening after dinner if that works.

subject: Re: Design Review Request – Kitchen Renovation Concepts
snippet: Wednesday evening works for me. Can you send over the files ahead of time so I c...
body:
Wednesday evening works for me. Can you send over the files ahead of time so I can at least get a first look before we sit down? I know I usually need a bit of time to process things visually before I have useful opinions.

From what you're describing, I'm already leaning toward the transitional one — open shelving sounds like it could get cluttered fast, but I'll reserve judgment until I actually see it.

subject: Re: Design Review Request – Kitchen Renovation Concepts
snippet: Attaching the PDFs now — there's one for each concept plus a shared materials sh...
body:
Attaching the PDFs now — there's one for each concept plus a shared materials sheet with the tile and cabinet finish options. The designer also included a rough timeline which I think is optimistic but worth reviewing.

And for the record, open shelving can look amazing if we're disciplined about what goes on it. But yes, I know us, so I'm not going to fight you too hard on that one. See you Wednesday.

Thank you for the reminder. We'll be there — Sarah and I have both already blocked the morning. Quick question: should Sarah take any different precautions in the week leading up to the baseline, or is the protocol just the standard? We want to make sure we go in prepared.

subject: Thanksgiving plans?
snippet: Just checking in. Dad and I were talking and wanted to know if you and Sarah are planning...
body:
Hey sweetie, just checking in. Dad and I were talking and wanted to know if you and Sarah are planning to come down for Thanksgiving. Dorothy has been asking about you — she has her good days and bad days but she perked up the other day when I mentioned your name.

No pressure if work is crazy, but it would mean a lot to all of us to have you here. Scott will be down from school too.

Love you,
Mom

subject: Re: Thanksgiving plans?
snippet: Yes — we're absolutely planning to be there for Thanksgiving. Sarah already put it on the calendar...
body:
Hi Mom, yes — we're absolutely planning to be there for Thanksgiving. Sarah already put it on the calendar. We'll probably drive down Wednesday evening to avoid the traffic.

Glad to hear Dorothy was having a good day. Tell her I said hi and that I'll bring her favorite tea — that Earl Grey she likes from the shop on Mass Ave.

Love you both. Talk soon.

— Brian

subject: mRNA-4157 analysis deadline moved up — action needed
snippet: Just a heads up — the mRNA-4157 interim analysis timeline has been moved up by two weeks...
body:
Brian,

Just a heads up — the mRNA-4157 interim analysis timeline has been moved up by two weeks. The new internal deadline for the biostatistics deliverable is February 12, 2027. Please flag any concerns with me by end of this week so we can adjust resource allocation if needed.

Also, the NEJM submission deadline is December 16, 2026 — make sure your analysis scripts are version-controlled and reproducible before then.

Best,
Rebecca

subject: Re: mRNA-4157 analysis deadline moved up — action needed
snippet: February 12 is tight but manageable if we lock scope by end of this month...
body:
Hi Rebecca,

Thanks for the heads up. February 12 is tight but manageable if we lock scope by end of this month. My main concern is the missing follow-up data for the 12-week cohort — about 8% of participants still haven't had their final visit logged. I'll follow up with the CRO today.

I'll send you a more detailed status note by Friday.

Brian
